key_insight: The paper links retinal image phenotypes to systemic disease pathways, using graph-based models to separate local lesion evidence from same-subject pathway signals in diabetic retinopathy.
clinical_relevance: This could help prioritize which retinal features and systemic factors matter most for understanding and stratifying diabetic retinopathy risk, without replacing clinical evaluation.
method: Built an interpretable retinal phenotype (X1234) from vessel maps, lesion evidence, image embeddings, and AutoMorph biomarkers.
Used spatial and Jacobian branches to support retinal image interpretation and pathway prioritization.
Applied NHANES-based modeling with systemic exposures, covariates, and same-subject retinal mediators (R*) to summarize participant-level pathways.
evidence_signal: In the retinal fold, X1234 achieved 0.9055 binary DR accuracy, 0.9711 AUROC, and 0.8312 QWK for graded DR, while NHANES analysis highlighted HbA1c, urine albumin, pulse pressure, fasting glucose, and systolic blood pressure as the strongest anchors.
watch_item: The pathway signals are associative and model-dependent, so cross-cohort validation is needed before using them to infer causal mechanisms or guide decisions.
paper_title: Causal-RetiGraph: Cross-Cohort Retinal Support and Same-Subject Pathway Analysis for Diabetic Retinopathy
